text:
16. Once the issue relating to retrospective applicability of revised tariff 
has been upheld by the learned Single Judge and the writ petitions 
filed by the respondents were dismissed, against which intra-court 
appeals at the instance of the respondents were pending, the High 
Court ought not to have proceeded with the hearing of the writ petition. 
Rather, it should have awaited the outcome of the pending intra-
Court appeals relating to retrospective application of the notification 
dated 23.07.2010. Subject to the final outcome of the said intra-court 
appeals, the writ petition should have been decided. This was all the 
more necessary for the High Court in view of the consistent stand 
taken by the respondents that the demands raised by the appellant 
vide various notices issued prior to 12.08.2015 or thereafter may be 
deferred awaiting the outcome of the appeals. 
192 
Supreme Court Reports
